meta.title: Modal
meta.description: Overlay surface for focused tasks — short, contained flows with confirmation, light input, or contextual decisions.

hero.title: Modal
hero.desc:
Modal is an overlay surface used to deliver task-related information or to collect confirmation and light input from the user. The user must address what's inside before continuing, which helps focus attention on the current task.

Modal pairs with a Dim layer that disables background interaction — directing the user's attention to the task at hand. Because of this, Modal is best for short, focused work; avoid using it for long or complex flows.

When content volume grows or steps lengthen, prefer Drawer or a dedicated page over Modal.

anatomy.title: Anatomy
anatomy.caption:
1. Header
2. Text Description
3. Contents
4. Secondary Btn
5. Primary Btn

buttons.title: Buttons
buttons.desc: Modal supports one to three buttons, with two as the recommended default. The highest-priority button uses Green-500 text and sits in the leading position. A button that backs out of the flow must always be included.

criteria.title: Modality Criteria & Checklist
criteria.desc:
Use these criteria and the checklist when deciding whether to introduce a modal. Criteria split into Required and Recommended — Required must be served as a modal because they demand focused attention.

Modal in Modal: stacking modals makes it hard for users to retrace their steps and breaks the task context. When a second modal would otherwise appear, prefer a normal view unless the Required criteria apply.

*Task modal, *Confirmation modal

requiredRows.label: Task-orientation
requiredRows.desc: The modal task is immersive content (exception: full-screen experiences)

requiredRows.label: Completeness
requiredRows.desc: The modal task has a clear start point and end point

recommendRows.label: Context mismatch
recommendRows.desc: The modal task sits outside the existing context

recommendRows.label: Isolation
recommendRows.desc: The user should be prevented from navigating to other screens while the modal task is in progress

recommendRows.label: Non-primary
recommendRows.desc: The modal task is unrelated to the primary task of the host screen

recommendRows.label: Return
recommendRows.desc: The user must return to the host screen after completing the modal task

requiredCheckRows.label: Immersion
requiredCheckRows.desc: Does the surface provide enough focus to complete the task?

requiredCheckRows.label: Completeness
requiredCheckRows.desc: Does the task run from start to finish on a single linear flow?

recommendCheckRows.label: Context mismatch
recommendCheckRows.desc: The modal task sits outside the existing context
Can the user grasp the volume of information on the entry screen at a glance?
Is the entry menu structure simple rather than nested?
Does another modal rarely appear after entering this one?

recommendCheckRows.label: Isolation
recommendCheckRows.desc: Is there a clear benefit to limiting navigation until the task is completed or canceled?

recommendCheckRows.label: Non-primary
recommendCheckRows.desc: Is there a clear benefit to limiting navigation until the task is completed or canceled?

recommendCheckRows.label: Return
recommendCheckRows.desc: Must the user return to the host screen after completing the task?

scrim.title: Scrim Area (Overlay)
scrim.desc: The Dim layer is a transparent surface that separates the background from the Modal. It signals that the rest of the screen isn't reachable and focuses attention on the modal. Overlay uses Black at 60% opacity. Clicking the Dim area doesn't return the user to the host screen — surface a Cancel button for that path.

spacing.title: Spacing
spacing.desc: Modal's default width is 448px; both Width and Height are flexible. The contents area uses 24px or 32px margin on all sides depending on Modal size and content. The gap between Header and Contents Area is 24px. Within Contents Area, the typical gap is 16px (24px when needed). The gap between Contents Area and the Footer button row is 32px. Buttons use Medium size at 40px. Modal scroll begins when the content occupies more than 84% of the viewport.

font.title: Font
font.desc: Modal uses Inter. Heading: H2 (Inter / Semibold / 24 / Gray50). Subtitle: Subtitle1 (Inter / Medium / 16 / Gray400). Body: Body2 (Inter / Regular / 14 / Gray700).

contentAction.title: Content & Action Area
contentAction.desc:
Modal separates content from action. The footer action area uses right-aligned horizontal buttons by default — Primary + Secondary combinations are recommended, and groups of three or more should be avoided. A Dismiss action that closes the Modal must always be included.

When content exceeds the max height (or the configured height), only the body area scrolls while the action area stays fixed. This keeps primary actions reachable while users review long content.
